response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with a strong software craftsmanship ethos, but he falls critically short of the role's hard requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. His primary languages are TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. While he has touched distributed systems and high-throughput services at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, there is no evidence of production cloud platform ownership, cloud compute architecture, or technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Hands-on exposure to high-throughput distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), demonstrating comfort with low-latency, large-scale backend systems.
Consistent software craftsmanship culture: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and community knowledge-sharing via workshops and talks.
Staff-level seniority across multiple employers, with cross-functional impact including DevOps infrastructure setup (GitLab, CI servers, Docker, Kubernetes).
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months (NI LabWindows role) plus a thesis project — far below the 8-year hard minimum; C++ is absent from his own skills list.
No demonstrated cloud compute platform ownership or production cloud architecture; AWS exposure is limited to CDK usage in a pricing-team product role.
No evidence of technical leadership specifically mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, which is both a preferred qualification and core to the role's day-to-day responsibilities.
